questions.q: Tipu Sultan was the ruler of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan was the ruler of the Kingdom of Mysore in southern India.

questions.q: By what nickname is Tipu Sultan famously known?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan is known as the 'Tiger of Mysore' for his fierce resistance to the British.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan was the son of which Mysore ruler?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan was the son of Hyder Ali, the de facto ruler of Mysore.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan is famous for pioneering the military use of what weapon made of iron?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan pioneered the military use of iron-cased rockets, advancing the technology used by his father.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan died in 1799 defending which capital city?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan died in 1799 defending his capital, Srirangapatna (Seringapatam), against the British.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan resisted the expansion of which European power in southern India?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan fiercely resisted British expansion in southern India.

questions.q: In which year did Tipu Sultan die?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan died in 1799 during the Fourth Anglo-Mysore War.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan became ruler of Mysore in which year?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan became ruler of Mysore in 1782 following the death of his father Hyder Ali.

questions.q: The animal that became Tipu Sultan's personal emblem and motif was the what?
questions.explanation: The tiger was Tipu Sultan's emblem, reflected in his nickname and in objects like the famous 'Tipu's Tiger' automaton.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan fought a series of wars against the British known by what name?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan fought the British in the Anglo-Mysore Wars, dying in the fourth of them.

questions.q: In which war did Tipu Sultan die in 1799?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan died at the fall of Srirangapatna during the Fourth Anglo-Mysore War in 1799.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan continued and concluded which war begun by his father, signing the Treaty of Mangalore in 1784?
questions.explanation: Tipu concluded the Second Anglo-Mysore War with the Treaty of Mangalore in 1784 after Hyder Ali's death.

questions.q: The Third Anglo-Mysore War ended in 1792 with which treaty, in which Tipu ceded much territory?
questions.explanation: The Third Anglo-Mysore War ended with the 1792 Treaty of Seringapatam, forcing Tipu to cede half his territory.

questions.q: Which British Governor-General led the campaign that ended in Tipu Sultan's death in 1799?
questions.explanation: Lord Wellesley (the Marquess Wellesley) directed the campaign of 1799 that ended at Srirangapatna with Tipu's death.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan sought foreign alliances against the British, notably reaching out to which power led by Napoleon's nation?
questions.explanation: Tipu sought an alliance with France, even corresponding regarding Napoleon's Egyptian campaign, alarming the British.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan introduced reforms in Mysore including a new what?
options: System of caste abolition fully enforced
questions.explanation: Tipu introduced a new calendar, coinage, and administrative measures as part of his reforms in Mysore.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan promoted the planting and trade of which crop, founding a state commercial venture?
questions.explanation: Tipu is credited with promoting sericulture (silk production) in Mysore, an industry that endures in the region.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan's rockets were later studied by the British and influenced the development of which weapon system?
questions.explanation: Captured Mysorean rockets influenced William Congreve, who developed the British Congreve rocket.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan built a summer palace and gardens at which site near Srirangapatna?
questions.explanation: Tipu's summer palace, the Daria Daulat Bagh, stands at Srirangapatna and is known for its murals.

questions.q: The famous mechanical automaton 'Tipu's Tiger', now in a London museum, depicts a tiger mauling whom?
questions.explanation: 'Tipu's Tiger' shows a tiger savaging a European soldier and is held in the Victoria and Albert Museum, London.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan was buried at the Gumbaz in Srirangapatna alongside whom?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan is buried in the Gumbaz mausoleum at Srirangapatna beside his father Hyder Ali.

questions.q: Against which neighbouring kingdom's attack did Tipu Sultan launch the campaign that triggered the Third Anglo-Mysore War?
questions.explanation: Tipu's attack on Travancore, a British ally, helped trigger the Third Anglo-Mysore War.

questions.q: In approximately which year was Tipu Sultan born?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan was born around 1750 (commonly given as 1751) at Devanahalli.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan was born at which town near present-day Bengaluru?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan was born at Devanahalli, north of present-day Bengaluru.

questions.q: Under the 1792 Treaty of Seringapatam, Tipu was forced to surrender two of his sons to the British as what?
questions.explanation: The 1792 treaty required Tipu to hand over two sons as hostages until he paid the war indemnity.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan styled his throne and regalia with which animal-head motif, symbolising his power?
questions.explanation: Tipu's throne and arms bore tiger heads and the tiger-stripe 'bubri' motif, reinforcing his Tiger of Mysore identity.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan sent diplomatic embassies abroad seeking alliances, including a notable mission to the court of which ruler?
questions.explanation: Tipu sent an embassy to the Ottoman Sultan, as well as to France, seeking support against the British.

questions.q: During the final assault of 1799, the British were aided by intelligence and the defection associated with which of Tipu's officials?
questions.explanation: Tipu's minister Mir Sadiq is traditionally accused of treachery aiding the British during the fall of Srirangapatna.

questions.q: After Tipu's death in 1799, the British restored which dynasty to the throne of Mysore as a princely state?
questions.explanation: Following Tipu's defeat, the British reinstated the Wodeyar dynasty to a reduced princely state of Mysore.

questions.q: Tipu Sultan renamed his capital and styled his realm with a title reflecting his religious devotion. He called his government by what name meaning 'Gift of God'?
questions.explanation: Tipu referred to his domain as Khudadad ('God-given'), styling his state the Khudadad Sarkar.
